ideation: This animation sequence will depict the octopus's eight arms moving in a playful, almost dancing manner.  The robot, lacking eight arms, will simulate this with its two arms performing a series of expressive, fluid movements that evoke the octopus's tentacle-like appendages. We'll focus on creating a sense of flow and movement, mimicking the octopus's graceful underwater dance. The sequence will emphasize dynamic changes in arm positions between frames.  Each arm will move independently, creating a sense of fluidity and organic movement that contrasts with the robot's rigid structure. Frame 1 will set the stage with both arms in a relaxed pose. Frame 2 will have one arm moving upward and outward in a wide arc, while the other moves downward and inward, creating a sense of reaching and retracting. Frame 3 will reverse this motion, suggesting a fluid, almost hypnotic movement. Frame 4 will showcase both arms moving together in a circular motion, simulating a tentacle wrapping around something. Frame 5 will bring the arms back to rest. This sequence uses a combination of joint1 rotations and joint2 pitches to create the sweeping motions of the octopus arms. The wrists will rotate slightly to add expressiveness.

frames.beat: both arms rest, ready to unfurl
arms reach out and curl in opposite directions
the arms sweep and retract in a fluid exchange
both arms circle together, a playful tentacle dance
arms return to a resting, relaxed state
